meta.tagline: One Prompt → Fully Operational Software Company

strategic_plan.business_objective: Build a multi-agent orchestration platform that transforms a single user prompt into a production-ready software application with CI/CD, containerization, Kubernetes deployment, and monitoring
target_users: Enterprise innovation labs prototyping new products

ideal_customer_profile.primary: Technical founders who can evaluate output but lack time/team to build end-to-end
ideal_customer_profile.secondary: Non-technical founders with basic technical literacy
ideal_customer_profile.tertiary: Engineering teams automating boilerplate and infra setup

revenue_model.team_tier: $199/month - unlimited projects, custom agents, private repos
revenue_model.enterprise_tier: Custom pricing - self-hosted, SSO, audit logs

go_to_market.phase_2: Product Hunt launch with demo video
go_to_market.phase_3: Content marketing - blog posts showing prompt-to-production workflows
go_to_market.phase_4: Enterprise outreach via LinkedIn and direct sales

constraints: v1 locked to Node.js + MongoDB + Next.js + Redis stack
No arbitrary shell execution outside sandboxed containers
Maximum 3 retry loops per failing component
Hard token budget cap per project generation
All agent outputs must be structured JSON
non_goals: Supporting every programming language in v1

success_metrics.iteration_cycles: <= 3 average retry cycles before stable build

features.name: Prompt Intake & Validation
features.description: Accept user prompt, validate intent, extract requirements using NLP
user_stories: As a user, I can enter a single prompt describing my app idea
As a user, I receive structured feedback if my prompt is too vague
As a user, I can see extracted requirements before generation begins
acceptance_criteria: Prompt must be >= 10 characters
System extracts: app type, target users, core features, auth needs
User can confirm or edit extracted requirements

features.name: Agent Orchestration Engine
features.description: Sequential execution of all 11 agents with structured I/O
user_stories: As the system, I execute agents in deterministic order
As a user, I can see which agent is currently active
As a user, I can see real-time progress of each phase
acceptance_criteria: Agents execute in defined phase order
Each agent receives structured input and returns structured JSON
Failed agents retry up to 3 times before escalation
Execution state persisted to MongoDB between phases

features.name: Code Generation Engine
features.description: File-by-file code generation for backend and frontend
user_stories: As a user, I receive individually generated source files
As a user, I can preview generated code before building
As a user, the generated code follows best practices
acceptance_criteria: Each file generated independently with proper imports
Generated code passes ESLint and TypeScript checks
Folder structure matches architect agent output
Environment variables extracted to .env template

features.name: Build & Test Pipeline
features.description: Automated build, dependency installation, and test execution in sandbox
user_stories: As a user, my generated app is automatically built and tested
As a user, I see build errors with suggested fixes
As a user, failed builds auto-retry with fixes applied
acceptance_criteria: Docker sandbox executes npm install + build
Test suite runs automatically after build
Build errors captured and fed back to developer agent
Maximum 3 retry cycles

features.name: Infrastructure Generation
features.description: Generate Docker, CI/CD, and Kubernetes configs
user_stories: As a user, I receive production-ready Dockerfiles
As a user, I receive GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines
As a user, I receive Kubernetes deployment manifests
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile uses multi-stage build with non-root user
CI pipeline includes lint, test, build, push stages
K8s manifests include Deployment, Service, Ingress, HPA, ConfigMap, Secret template

features.name: Project Dashboard
features.description: Web UI showing project status, agent progress, build logs, and generated output
user_stories: As a user, I can see all my projects in a dashboard
As a user, I can view real-time agent execution progress
As a user, I can browse generated files and download the project
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard lists all user projects with status
Real-time progress via WebSocket or SSE
File browser with syntax highlighting
Download as .zip or push to GitHub

features.name: GitHub Integration
features.description: Auto-create repo, commit generated code, configure branch strategy
user_stories: As a user, I can connect my GitHub account
As a user, the system auto-creates a repo for my project
As a user, generated code is committed per sprint
acceptance_criteria: OAuth-based GitHub authentication
Repo created with proper .gitignore and README
Code committed with meaningful commit messages
Branch protection rules suggested

features.name: Observability Setup
features.description: Generate monitoring, logging, and alerting configuration
user_stories: As a user, my generated app includes health check endpoints
As a user, I receive Prometheus metrics configuration
As a user, I receive structured logging setup
acceptance_criteria: Health check endpoint at /health and /ready
Prometheus metrics at /metrics
Structured JSON logging with correlation IDs
K8s liveness and readiness probes configured

edge_cases: User prompt is a single word (e.g., 'Twitter')
MongoDB connection fails during sandbox build
Concurrent project generation by same user

sprints.name: Foundation Sprint
sprints.goal: Core backend API, auth, database models, project CRUD
tasks: Write unit tests for auth and project endpoints

sprints.name: Orchestration Sprint
sprints.goal: Agent orchestration engine, prompt processing, phase execution
tasks: Build prompt intake and validation service
Build phase state machine with Redis

sprints.name: Code Generation Sprint
sprints.goal: Developer agents, code generation, file output
tasks: Implement template system for generated code
Build token-aware chunking for large projects
Generate Mongoose models from architect schema
Generate Express routes from API spec
Generate Next.js pages from frontend plan
Write generated code to project workspace

sprints.name: Build & Test Sprint
sprints.goal: Sandbox execution, build pipeline, test runner, self-healing loop
tasks: Build self-healing loop (error capture → fix → retry)
Implement DevOps Agent (Dockerfile, CI/CD, K8s generation)
Write integration tests for full pipeline

sprints.name: Frontend Dashboard Sprint
sprints.goal: Next.js dashboard, project management UI, real-time progress
tasks: Initialize Next.js frontend with App Router
Build generated file browser with syntax highlighting

sprints.name: Infrastructure & Launch Sprint
sprints.goal: Docker, CI/CD, K8s, monitoring, production deployment
tasks: Write Docker Compose for local dev

architecture_design.rationale: Single deployable unit with clear module boundaries. Avoids microservice overhead for v1 while maintaining clean separation for future extraction.

fields.name: String
fields.tokenUsage: { used: Number, limit: Number, resetAt: Date }

fields.name: String
fields.outputs: { strategic_plan: Object, prd: Object, architecture: Object, backend_plan: Object, frontend_plan: Object, testing_plan: Object, devops_plan: Object, security_plan: Object, observability_plan: Object }
fields.generatedFiles: [{ path: String, content: String, language: String }]
fields.buildLog: [{ timestamp: Date, level: String, message: String }]

collections.name: agent_executions
collections.purpose: Audit log of every agent execution

fields.tokenUsage: { prompt: Number, completion: Number, total: Number }

collections.name: templates
collections.purpose: Code templates for different file types

fields.name: String
fields.category: String (enum: model, controller, route, middleware, component, page, config)

database.schema_validation: Mongoose schema-level validation with Zod for API input

usage.job_queue: Bull queue for agent execution jobs. Each project creates a job chain: phase1 → phase2 → ... → phase9
usage.caching: Cache LLM responses for identical prompts (TTL: 24h). Cache project status for dashboard polling.
usage.session_store: Redis-backed session store for WebSocket connections
usage.rate_limiting: Sliding window rate limiter per user per endpoint
usage.execution_state: Real-time phase/agent state for SSE progress streaming

auth_architecture.storage: Access token in memory, refresh token in httpOnly cookie

security_model.input_validation: Zod schemas on all API endpoints

error_handling.response_format: { success: false, error: { code: string, message: string, details?: object } }

frontend_plan.state_management: React Context + SWR for server state

pages.description: Landing page with product info and CTA
New project creation with prompt input
Project detail - agent progress, file browser, logs

auth_handling.strategy: httpOnly cookie for refresh token, in-memory access token
auth_handling.protected_routes: Middleware-based route protection via Next.js middleware.ts
auth_handling.token_refresh: Automatic refresh on 401 response via Axios interceptor

targets: Full auth flow (register → login → refresh → logout)

edge_cases: Concurrent project creation by same user
File browser with deeply nested paths

docker.backend_dockerfile: Multi-stage: node:20-alpine build → node:20-alpine production, non-root user, health check
docker.frontend_dockerfile: Multi-stage: node:20-alpine build → node:20-alpine with standalone Next.js output, non-root user
docker.docker_compose: Services: backend, frontend, mongodb, redis. Volumes for data persistence. Network isolation.

jwt_hardening.refresh_rotation: New refresh token on each refresh

input_validation.strategy: Validate all request bodies, params, and query strings at middleware level

mongo_injection.strategy: Sanitize all incoming request data, strip $ and . from keys

rate_limiting.strategy: Sliding window per IP and per user, stricter limits on auth endpoints

cors.strategy: Whitelist frontend origin only, no wildcard in production

headers.config: Default helmet config with CSP tailored to Next.js

dependency_audit.strategy: npm audit in CI pipeline, fail build on critical vulnerabilities

sandbox_isolation.strategy: Docker containers with no network access, resource limits, read-only filesystem except /tmp

logging.levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal

custom_metrics: http_requests_total (counter) - labeled by method, path, status
llm_tokens_used_total (counter) - labeled by agent name, type (prompt/completion)

health_checks.liveness: GET /health - returns 200 if process is alive
health_checks.readiness: GET /ready - returns 200 if MongoDB and Redis are connected

tracing.strategy: Request ID (UUID) generated per request, passed through all service calls and log entries

rules: MongoDBDown: health check failing for 1 minute
RedisDown: readiness check failing for 1 minute
HighTokenUsage: daily token usage > 80% of budget

risks.risk: LLM token costs exceed budget per project
risks.mitigation: Hard token cap per project, cached responses for similar prompts, chunked generation with early termination

risks.risk: Generated code fails to compile consistently
risks.mitigation: Self-healing loop with max 3 retries, template-based generation for boilerplate, validation before file write
Hard retry limit of 3 per agent, circuit breaker pattern, structured error escalation

risks.risk: Sandbox container escape or resource abuse
risks.mitigation: Docker containers with no network, resource limits (CPU, memory, disk), read-only filesystem, non-root user

risks.risk: MongoDB injection via generated code or user input
risks.mitigation: express-mongo-sanitize on all inputs, Mongoose schema validation, Zod input schemas

risks.risk: Single point of failure in orchestrator
risks.mitigation: BullMQ job persistence in Redis, job recovery on restart, phase state stored in MongoDB

risks.risk: LLM API rate limits or downtime
risks.mitigation: Retry with exponential backoff, queue-based execution smooths request rate, fallback model configuration

assumptions: Claude API (or compatible LLM API) is available and supports structured JSON output
Users have GitHub accounts for repo integration features
MongoDB Atlas or self-hosted MongoDB 7.x is available
Redis Stack 7.x is available (for BullMQ job queues)
Docker is available for sandbox execution of generated code
Kubernetes cluster is pre-provisioned for deployment
Container registry (Docker Hub, ECR, or GCR) is available for image storage
DNS and TLS certificates are managed externally
v1 generates only Node.js + MongoDB + Next.js applications
LLM token budget is tracked but not automatically billed in v1
Self-healing loop is limited to code fixes, not architecture changes
GitHub integration uses OAuth App (not GitHub App) in v1
